content.thinking:
The current node cert expires in 2 days (Jun 25). I need to:
1. Generate new node.key and node.crt with ~1 year validity using the existing CA key and cert
2. Also regenerate the client.root cert if needed
3. Update the Kubernetes secret with the new certificates
4. Rolling restart the CockroachDB pods to pick up the new certs

Let me proceed:
1. Generate new node key and CSR in the openssl-toolbox pod
2. Sign the CSR with the CA key/cert for 365 days
3. Update the Kubernetes secret with the new certs, then rolling restart the pods to apply them.

content.text: The current node cert expires in 2 days. I'll generate a new node cert valid for 1 year using the CA in the toolbox pod, then update the secret and rolling-restart the cluster.

input.command:
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- sh -c "
  openssl genrsa -out /tmp/certs/node.key.new 2048 2>&1 && echo 'KEY OK'
" 2>&1

kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- sh -c "
  openssl req -new -key /tmp/certs/node.key.new \
    -out /tmp/certs/node.csr.new \
    -config /tmp/certs/node.cnf 2>&1 && echo 'CSR OK'
" 2>&1
input.description: Generate new node CSR using existing node.cnf

input.command:
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- sh -c "
  openssl x509 -req \
    -in /tmp/certs/node.csr.new \
    -CA /tmp/certs/ca.crt \
    -CAkey /tmp/certs/ca.key \
    -CAcreateserial \
    -out /tmp/certs/node.crt.new \
    -days 365 \
    -extensions v3_req \
    -extfile /tmp/certs/node.cnf 2>&1 && echo 'CERT OK'
" 2>&1
input.description: Sign new node cert with CA for 365 days

content.thinking:
The new cert is valid until Jun 23, 2027 - that's ~1 year. Now I need to:
1. Extract the new cert and key from the pod
2. Update the Kubernetes secret with the new node.crt and node.key

Let me extract them and update the secret.

content.text: New cert is valid until Jun 23, 2027. Now I'll extract it and update the Kubernetes secret.

input.command:
NODE_CRT=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- cat /tmp/certs/node.crt.new | base64 -w0)
NODE_KEY=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- cat /tmp/certs/node.key.new | base64 -w0)
echo "CRT length: ${#NODE_CRT}"
echo "KEY length: ${#NODE_KEY}"
input.description: Extract new cert and key as base64

input.command:
NODE_CRT=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- cat /tmp/certs/node.crt.new | base64 -w0)
NODE_KEY=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- cat /tmp/certs/node.key.new | base64 -w0)

kubectl -n cockroachdb patch secret crdb-cluster-certs \
  --type='json' \
  -p="[{\"op\":\"replace\",\"path\":\"/data/node.crt\",\"value\":\"${NODE_CRT}\"},{\"op\":\"replace\",\"path\":\"/data/node.key\",\"value\":\"${NODE_KEY}\"}]" 2>&1
input.description: Patch the secret with new node cert and key

input.command:
# Verify the secret was updated with the new cert's fingerprint
kubectl -n cockroachdb get secret crdb-cluster-certs -o jsonpath='{.data.node\.crt}' | base64 -d | \
  openssl x509 -noout -fingerprint -sha256 2>&1
input.description: Verify secret now has the new cert

content.thinking:
The fingerprint matches what we generated. Now I need to do a rolling restart of the CockroachDB pods so they pick up the new certificates from the secret.

CockroachDB mounts the certs from the secret, so I need to restart the pods. Let me do a rolling restart one at a time to avoid quorum loss.

content.text: Secret updated. Now I'll rolling-restart the pods one at a time so they pick up the new certs without losing quorum.

input.command:
# Wait for crdb-cluster-0 to be running again
for i in $(seq 1 30); do
  STATUS=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb get pod crdb-cluster-0 --no-headers 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $3}')
  READY=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb get pod crdb-cluster-0 --no-headers 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $2}')
  echo "[$i] crdb-cluster-0: $STATUS ($READY)"
  if [ "$STATUS" = "Running" ] && [ "$READY" = "1/1" ]; then
    echo "Pod is ready"
    break
  fi
  sleep 5
done
